sentence_reviews.text: Large language models are high-capacity sequence models pretrained to predict or reconstruct language and adapted for generation, reasoning, retrieval, and tool use.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Transformer, scaling-law, and compute-optimal LLM sources directly support high-capacity pretrained language models and adaptation/use contexts.
sentence_reviews.notes: The definition is broad and matches established LLM literature.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Large language models is to connect Decoder-only causal transformers, Large-scale pretraining to downstream capabilities including Conversational assistants & chatbots, Code models, copilots & repository intelligence, Automated theorem proving & mathematical AI.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: The upstream/downstream role is inferred from the dependency graph and cited background sources.
sentence_reviews.notes: Useful graph synthesis, not directly established as an exact claim by the cited sources.

sentence_reviews.text: End-to-end quality is bounded by the weakest data, model, tool, interface, policy, infrastructure, and human-oversight component. Its direct dependencies include Decoder-only causal transformers, Large-scale pretraining.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: The generic end-to-end quality limitation is plausible for deployed systems, while the dependency list is graph-derived.
sentence_reviews.notes: Needs a reliability or systems-evaluation source for exact verification; dependency list portion is structural metadata.
